title: Sutera huillana
taxonomy: Sutera huillana is a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. It is native to Angola and is an annual or short-lived perennial herb. The classification of Sutera huillana includes its placement in the genus Sutera and its further classification within the family Scrophulariaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as figworts. The Scrophulariaceae family is part of the order Lamiales, which is a large and diverse order of flowering plants. The classification of Sutera huillana reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.

It is said to have various health benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. intro: Sutera huillana, also known as African mint, is a plant species belonging to the family Scrophulariaceae, native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Sutera huillana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Sutera huillana include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive discomfort.
